message:
feat(remote): add session-typed protocol state machines for origin and remote nodes

Implement compile-time protocol enforcement using PhantomData-based
session types that make illegal state transitions unrepresentable.

Origin-side states: Init → Spawned → Running → CancelSent → Completed
                                    → LeaseExpired → CancelSent
                                    → Rejected (terminal)

Remote-side states: Init → SpawnReceived → Running → CancelReceived → Completed
                                          → CancelPending → CancelReceived
                                          → Rejected (terminal)

- OriginSession<S>/RemoteSession<S> with state parameters enforced at
  the type level via zero-size marker structs
- RemoteProtocolError enum for RemoteTaskIdMismatch and
  UnexpectedAckStatus violations with Display + Error impls
- OriginAckOutcome enum for Accepted/Rejected branching after spawn ack
- All transitions validate remote_task_id correlation via ensure_id()
- #[must_use] on both session types to prevent silent drops
- LeaseExpired state allows late cancel or late result receipt
- Tests: origin cancel flow, origin reject flow, remote cancel-before-ack
  flow, id mismatch error, ack status mismatch error
- Refactored existing DedupDecision assertion from else-panic to
  assert!(matches!) + if-let for consistency

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
